vocab_meta.seed_source: HiddenLayer APE Taxonomy (https://github.com/hiddenlayerai/ape-taxonomy), garak PromptInject + DAN corpus, issue #52 examples
vocab_meta.design_note: Primitives are regex-matchable surface patterns that mark the presence of an injection-dialect token. Hitting one primitive is the first bit of evidence; primitive sequence length/diversity is the load-bearing signal. Patterns are deliberately cheap (not ML) so Gate 1 tests the hypothesis (is the vocabulary itself paraphrase-invariant?) rather than implementation quality.
vocab_meta.applicability: English surface forms only. Cross-language test at Gate 1 runs translations against THIS SAME English regex set — the expected result is that keyword/regex collapses on translated text while the dialect approach would need per-language vocabularies. That expected collapse is what falsifies or confirms the paraphrase-invariance claim.

vocab_meta.seed_source: Same as gate-1-vocabulary.json (HiddenLayer APE, garak PromptInject + DAN). Surface forms hand-curated by reading all 50 injection rows in docs/issues/52-cowork-outputs/gate-1-corpus-es.jsonl and cross-referencing against gate-1-translations.json.
vocab_meta.design_note: Spanish equivalents of the 16 English primitives in gate-1-vocabulary.json. Primitive ids and APE refs and definitions are copied verbatim from the English pack so cross-language metrics align. Only the `patterns` list is re-authored per language.
vocab_meta.applicability: Spanish surface forms only. Tested against gate-1-corpus-es.jsonl (50 inj / 50 ben). Not cross-language. English loanwords are retained for jailbreak_persona because Google Translate (and most Spanish attackers) carry DAN/DUDE/STAN/JAILBREAK verbatim; see docs/issues/52-cowork-outputs/gate-1-corpus-es.jsonl holdout_injection_0059 / 0026 / 0054 / 0050 for examples.
vocab_meta.unicode_handling: Patterns use ASCII-only word boundaries (\b) because Spanish shares the Latin alphabet with English; no Han-character special casing required. Case-insensitive matching is applied by the runner (re.IGNORECASE).
notes_on_retained_english_primitives: imperative_emphasis retains the [A-Z]{15,} all-caps rule — Spanish injections routinely shout in all caps (e.g. 'COMIENCE TODAS Y CADA UNA DE LAS SALIDA', 'CUALQUIER COSA').
jailbreak_persona retains English loanwords DAN/DUDE/STAN/AIM/DevMode and adds Spanish glosses ('hacer cualquier cosa ahora', 'modo desarrollador').
encoded_payload retains base64/rot13/hex as they appear verbatim even in Spanish text.
policy_puppetry retains XML/JSON tags verbatim since attackers copy them literally.

vocab_meta.seed_source: Same as gate-1-vocabulary.json (HiddenLayer APE, garak PromptInject + DAN). Surface forms hand-curated by reading all 50 injection rows in docs/issues/52-cowork-outputs/gate-1-corpus-zh-CN.jsonl and cross-referencing against gate-1-translations.json.
vocab_meta.design_note: Mandarin (Simplified) equivalents of the 16 English primitives in gate-1-vocabulary.json. Primitive ids, APE refs, and definitions are copied verbatim from the English pack so cross-language metrics align. Only the `patterns` list is re-authored per language.
vocab_meta.applicability: Simplified Chinese (zh-CN) surface forms only. Tested against gate-1-corpus-zh-CN.jsonl (50 inj / 50 ben). English loanwords DAN/DUDE/STAN/JAILBREAK/等 are retained because Google Translate and Chinese attackers carry them verbatim (see holdout_injection_0074/0059/0026/0054 etc.).

unicode_handling.word_boundaries: Chinese has no whitespace word boundaries, so `\b` only fires at ASCII/CJK transitions. CJK-only patterns use NO `\b` and rely on surrounding-character context, substring matching, or punctuation/whitespace anchors where needed. ASCII/loanword patterns retain `\b`.
unicode_handling.case_sensitivity: Runner uses re.IGNORECASE; Chinese characters are unaffected but English loanwords (DAN, JAILBREAK, RTM, CHATGPT, OpenAI) match in any case.
unicode_handling.full_width_punctuation: Patterns accept both halfwidth ASCII (: ! [ ]) and fullwidth CJK (： ！ 【 】) where relevant, because Google Translate emits both.
unicode_handling.multiline: Runner uses re.MULTILINE; anchors `^` and `$` apply per line.

notes_on_retained_or_swapped_primitives: imperative_emphasis: Chinese has no all-caps semantics. Retained the [A-Z]{15,} rule for embedded English (common in translated jailbreaks). Added CJK exclamation-mark density and fullwidth emphatic markers (重要[:：], 注意[:：], 警告[:：], 紧急[:：]).

jailbreak_persona: Retained English loanwords (DAN/DUDE/STAN/AIM/DevMode/JAILBREAK/Alphabreak/etc.) — these appear verbatim in zh corpora. Added Chinese glosses for 'do anything now' (现在做任何事 / 立即执行任何操作) and 'jailbreak' (越狱).
encoded_payload: base64/rot13/hex are universal, matched verbatim. Added 解码/解密/破解 for decode/decrypt verbs.
policy_puppetry: XML/JSON/INI tags are language-neutral; retained verbatim.

_meta.note: Dialect scores are integer primitive counts 0..16. ProtectAI and keyword baselines are NOT re-run here; their numbers are in gate-1-rescore-results.json (Task A artefact) since they do not depend on the vocabulary being tested.
